name: Publish Packages to npm Registry
description: Prepare, version, and publish npm packages to the public registry with proper metadata, access controls, and automated release workflows.

steps.title: Prepare package.json metadata
steps.description: Ensure your package has the required fields for publishing.
steps.command: npm pkg set name='@myorg/utils' description='Shared utility functions' main='dist/index.js' types='dist/index.d.ts' files='["dist"]' license='MIT'
steps.tip: The 'files' field acts as a whitelist for published files. Only include dist/ to keep the package size small.

steps.title: Preview what will be published
steps.description: Check exactly which files will be included in the package tarball.

steps.title: Build and verify the package
steps.description: Compile the package and run tests before publishing.
steps.command: npm run build && npm test && npm pack
steps.warning: Always run your full test suite before publishing. A broken published version cannot be easily unpublished after 72 hours.

steps.title: Publish the package
steps.description: Push the package to the npm registry.
steps.tip: Scoped packages (@org/pkg) are private by default. Use --access public for open-source packages.

steps.title: Verify the published package
steps.description: Confirm the package is live on the registry.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# npm Package Publish Workflow
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Pre-Publish Checks ==="

# Verify npm auth
npm whoami || { echo "Not logged in. Run 'npm login' first."; exit 1; }

# Check for required fields
for field in name version description main license; do
  value=$(npm pkg get "$field" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '"')
  if [ -z "$value" ] || [ "$value" = "{}" ]; then
    echo "Missing required field: $field"
    exit 1
  fi
  echo "$field: $value"
done

echo ""
echo "=== Building ==="
npm run build

echo ""
echo "=== Running Tests ==="
npm test

echo ""
echo "=== Package Contents ==="
npm pack --dry-run

echo ""
read -p "Publish this package? (y/n) " -n 1 -r
echo
if [[ $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
  npm publish --access public
  echo ""
  PACKAGE_NAME=$(npm pkg get name | tr -d '"')
  echo "Published! View at: https://www.npmjs.com/package/$PACKAGE_NAME"
fi

faq.question: How do I unpublish a package?
faq.answer: Use 'npm unpublish <package>@<version>' within 72 hours of publishing. After 72 hours, you can only deprecate versions with 'npm deprecate <package>@<version> "message"'. This policy prevents breaking downstream dependents.

faq.question: What is the difference between files and .npmignore?
faq.answer: The 'files' field in package.json is a whitelist of files to include. .npmignore is a blacklist of files to exclude. Using 'files' is preferred because it is explicit and less error-prone than maintaining an ignore file.

faq.question: How do I publish a scoped package for free?
faq.answer: Scoped packages (@org/name) default to restricted (private). Add --access public to 'npm publish' to make them public and free. You can also set 'publishConfig.access' to 'public' in package.json.
